Functions for early initialization and multitasking initialization of PULSE in the firmware.

# Purpose
This C header file declares two functions, [`pulse_early_init`](<#pulse_early_init>) and [`pulse_init`](<#pulse_init>), which are part of the initialization process for a system component named PULSE. The [`pulse_early_init`](<#pulse_early_init>) function is intended to perform necessary link-maintenance tasks and should be called early in the boot process, ideally after the initialization of debug serial output. The [`pulse_init`](<#pulse_init>) function is responsible for initializing the multitasking capabilities of PULSE. The file uses the `#pragma once` directive to prevent multiple inclusions during compilation.
